note: Found 2153 (91.4% of paper). Difference likely from BV-BRC database changes since 2022 PATRIC snapshot and different carbapenemase gene detection (API vs Kleborate).
Found 1484 (91.6%). Same data source difference.
Found 669 (90.9%). Same data source difference.

Found 955 (247% of paper). Increase because BV-BRC database has grown since 2022 AND paper applied sample source filters we cannot fully replicate from API metadata. Direction consistent: ST11 is very prevalent.

Clear increase in ST11 proportion from period 1 to period 2. Our numbers (23.2% → 91.2%) show the same trend. Higher absolute values because ST11 is better represented in current BV-BRC with MLST typing.

claim: China leading country for ST11 CRKP (64.51%, 249/386)
note: China at 61.0% — confirmed as dominant source. Very close to paper's 64.51%.
Brazil at 8.9% — very close to paper's 9.84%. Confirmed as second major source.
USA at 4.0% — lower than paper's 8.55%. Poland now appears at 7.5% in our data, possibly due to new submissions to BV-BRC since 2022.
KPC-2/KPC family found in 78.3% of ST11 CRKP genomes. Confirmed as dominant carbapenemase.
NDM-1 at 13.9% — confirmed as second most common carbapenemase in ST11 CRKP.
OXA-48 at 8.5% — confirmed as third most common. Higher percentage likely due to expanded BV-BRC data.
92% of genomes lack body_sample_site metadata in BV-BRC API. Cannot reliably test this claim. Among 76 genomes with source data, blood was 25 — consistent but insufficient sample.
K-locus (KL) serotyping requires running Kleborate on genome assemblies. BV-BRC API does not provide KL type annotations. Cannot test.
K-locus serotyping not available via BV-BRC API. Requires Kleborate.
K-locus serotyping not available via BV-BRC API.

claim: KL64 median 78 virulence genes vs KL47 median 63 (p<0.001)
note: Cannot test without KL type assignments. Also, BV-BRC virulence gene counts differ from Abricate+VFDB methodology.

claim: 35 differentially distributed virulence genes between KL47 and KL64
note: Cannot test without KL type assignments and matching VFDB analysis methodology.

claim: KL64 carries ~303bp more in wzc CD1-VR2-CD2 region (GC 58.14% vs 53.84%)
note: Requires genome sequence analysis of representative KL47 and KL64 strains. Not testable via API metadata alone.
Our top year is 2017 (27.1%), then 2016 (17.7%). Different from paper's 2015/2016 peak. BV-BRC has more recent submissions, shifting the distribution.

claim: 386 ST11 CRKP divide into 9 phylogenetic clades
note: Requires core genome alignment (Roary) and phylogenetic analysis (RAxML-ng). Not possible via API alone.
